changes.value:
The `search_entities` Tauri command (commands.rs line 352) accepts an arbitrary-length `query: String` with no upper bound check. While `dispatch_command` validates command IDs (max 128 chars, ASCII-only), `search_entities` passes the query directly to the fuzzy search engine.

For a desktop app this is low risk (the user controls input), but a very long query string could cause the fuzzy matcher to do excessive work. Consider adding a reasonable length cap (e.g. 500 chars) for consistency with the validation pattern used elsewhere.

Severity: Low (defense in depth)"
[Low] No input length limit on search_entities query parameter

For a desktop app this is low risk (the user controls input), but a very long query string could cause the fuzzy matcher to do excessive work. Consider adding a reasonable length cap (e.g. 500 chars) for consistency with the validation pattern used elsewhere.

Severity: Low (defense in depth)"
\ No newline at end of file
